subject: Geography
title: The Cenotes of Yucatán
description: A short lesson to help you apply The Cenotes of Yucatán.

hook.text: The Yucatán Peninsula has over 6,000 cenotes—sinkholes filled with crystal-clear water.

buildup.text: Limestone collapsed into underground rivers, creating natural swimming pools linked by tunnels.

discovery.text: The Maya considered cenotes sacred entrances to the underworld and made offerings there.

twist.text: A ring of cenotes traces the edge of the Chicxulub crater—the asteroid that killed the dinosaurs.

climax.text: A 66-million-year-old impact crater created the geology that the Maya called divine.

punchline.text: An asteroid killed the dinosaurs and carved the Mayan underworld.

quiz.question: What geological event created the ring of cenotes in Yucatán?
